title: Scenario
text: A company stores confidential customer records in an internal database protected by a firewall. The firewall is intended to prevent unauthorized users from reaching sensitive systems.

title: What Does a Firewall Do?
text: A firewall acts as a security barrier between trusted internal systems and external networks. It decides which network traffic is allowed or blocked based on predefined security rules.

title: Common Firewall Misconfigurations
text: Firewalls become ineffective when rules are too permissive, default configurations are left unchanged, unnecessary services remain exposed, or firewall rules are not reviewed regularly. These mistakes may unintentionally expose sensitive systems to external users.

title: Attacker Goal
text: The attacker wants to discover whether any protected systems have been unintentionally exposed through weak firewall configurations.

title: Try Again
text: Attackers typically do not have direct access to firewall configurations. Instead, they identify services that are unintentionally reachable because of incorrect firewall rules.

title: Unexpected Exposure
text: The attacker discovers that services intended to remain internal are accessible from outside the organization. This unintended exposure is caused by overly permissive firewall rules.

title: Unauthorized Access
text: Because the firewall allows unnecessary traffic, the attacker reaches systems that should have been protected from external access.

title: Database Compromise
text: Improper firewall configurations can allow confidential information to be viewed, modified, or deleted, threatening the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of organizational data.

title: How to Prevent This
text: Organizations should adopt a default-deny firewall policy, regularly review firewall rules, remove unnecessary exposed services, keep configurations updated, and use firewall management solutions such as FireMon and AlgoSec to simplify configuration management and reduce human error.

title: Attack Replay
text: Review how the attacker identified an exposed service, reached protected systems, and compromised sensitive data because of a firewall misconfiguration.

title: Scenario Complete
text: Firewalls are only effective when correctly configured. Even a single overly permissive rule can expose internal services, allowing attackers to reach systems that should never have been accessible.
